events.label: Berkshire buys into all four majors
events.detail: Warren Buffett's Berkshire Hathaway discloses big new stakes in American, Delta, and United — and Buffett says on TV that he's buying Southwest too. A famous stock-picker joins the everyone-owns-everyone club, and the purple line feels it.

events.label: COVID-19 collapses air travel
events.detail: US air travel falls ~95% at the worst point. Fares and route maps convulse; every line on this chart carries the scar.

events.label: Berkshire exits all airline stakes
events.detail: Buffett sells all four airline stakes in a single month: 'The world has changed for the airlines.' One of the biggest shared owners leaves the web — watch the purple line step down.
events.source: Berkshire Hathaway annual meeting, May 2, 2020; CNBC 2020-05-02

events.label: Frontier IPOs (Indigo keeps control)
events.detail: Frontier starts selling shares to the public, but the private investment firm Indigo Partners keeps control — index funds only ever get a sliver. Not every airline is wired into the web equally.

events.label: JetBlue–Spirit merger blocked
events.detail: A federal judge blocks JetBlue's $3.8B purchase of Spirit, ruling it would raise fares. That's ordinary, visible concentration — the kind regulators' score can see — and it leaves Spirit with no rescue plan.
events.source: US v. JetBlue, D. Mass., Jan 16 2024

events.label: Alaska absorbs Hawaiian
events.detail: Alaska buys Hawaiian outright — an ordinary merger, the kind HHI is built to catch. You can see it in the routes they shared.

events.label: Spirit files Chapter 11 (first)
events.detail: Spirit files for bankruptcy. The deal on the table: the airline can't pay $795M it owes, so the lenders will be paid in ownership of the company instead.

events.label: Spirit emerges — creditors own it now
events.detail: Spirit exits bankruptcy under new ownership — its old shareholders, index funds included, are wiped out, and the lenders (investment firms like Citadel and PIMCO) become the owners. Spirit leaves the ownership web while its planes keep flying. The index funds had already lost this one.

events.label: Spirit files Chapter 11 (second)
events.detail: Five months after escaping bankruptcy, Spirit is back in it — and this time the company is headed for shutdown, not rescue.

events.label: Jet-fuel price spike
events.detail: The Iran-war fuel spike raises costs on ALL routes — the confound the difference-in-differences design exists to remove.

events.label: Spirit Airlines ceases operations
events.detail: The natural experiment begins: a major low-cost carrier exits its entire network at once. Concentration jumps; the common-ownership term barely moves (Spirit was creditor-owned). The registered prediction starts its clock.

events.label: Buffett returns: Berkshire takes 6.1% of Delta
events.detail: Berkshire's filing reveals a $2.6B Delta stake — 6.1% of the airline, bought during the first quarter of 2026. The ownership web had begun rewiring itself even before the experiment could report.
events.source: Berkshire SC 13G on DAL, May 2026; CNBC 2026-05-15
